purpose: Running log of every failure, flag, and warning found during the build, for Isha to review. Maintained by Claude as tasks execute; entries are never deleted, only marked resolved.

severity_legend.FAILURE: something is broken or failing a check right now
severity_legend.FLAG: a decision or verification Isha must make before a later task depends on it
severity_legend.WARNING: a latent risk; not broken today, could bite later

entries.resolved: 2026-07-18 by Step 1 — Isha set SPEED_BENCH=10 + THRESHOLD=60
entries.task: i1 (discovered during), pre-existing on clean HEAD
entries.title: Offline demo fails its own acceptance checks (Page B parallel misses the 20% bar)
entries.detail: npm run demo reproducibly fails 2/6 checks: Page B's parallel fix measures only ~13-16% time-to-settled cut vs the 20% THRESHOLD (CoV ~1%, systematic). Verified pre-existing: clean HEAD (git stash) fails identically. Mechanism (audit-verified): SPEED_BENCH=40 compresses Page B's parallel settle model to 52.5ms, but ~75ms of fixed render/timer overhead per run (4x CPU throttle) swamps it; the 145ms baseline absorbs the overhead, so only parallel inflates and the delta collapses from a modeled 64% to ~15%. Page A survives because its parallel long-pole is 900ms vs Page B's 1900ms map.
options: Lower SPEED_BENCH from 40 to 10 (recommended: fixes the physics; demo runs ~4x longer)
Shrink Page B's map dur (changes the substrate story)
Lower THRESHOLD below 20% (moves the goalposts; touches Devansh's d4 ship number)
entries.action: Isha + Devansh decide calibration (ties to d4 'confirm the number'), then re-verify 6/6 PASS
entries.update_2026_07_18: RESOLVED. Isha approved: SPEED_BENCH=10 (dmvlist.jsx:16) and THRESHOLD raised 20->60 (loop.mjs:14) to match Devansh's d4 ship number (>=60% time-to-settled cut). Verified 3x back-to-back: 6/6 PASS every run. Page A parallel 75.0-76.7%, Page B parallel 63.2-63.4% (stable ~3pp margin over the 60% bar, NOT flaky), spinner -0.6% to +0.6% (correctly rejected). Baseline CoVs tight. NOTE for i8/monitoring: Page B's margin over 60% is only ~3pp — if the substrate or environment changes, re-verify Page B still clears 60%; the CoV gate protects against noise but not against a genuine margin shift.

entries.title: Canonical bench surface undecided: __benchfirst vs back-compat aliases
entries.detail: bench/harness.mjs consumes window.__runInteraction and window.__profile (the aliases), not window.__benchfirst directly. Both are live, but the locked contract should name one canonical. Decide (or update the harness) before i3/i6 build on it.
entries.action: Pick canonical surface before starting i3

entries.title: window.__dmv has zero code consumers — its stability is untested
entries.detail: Only README.md references __dmv; no script drives setInput/submit/getState. Also submit and submitAndSettle are identical closures (redundant surface member). Cheap test when a browser is open: setInput('vin','FAIL'); await submitAndSettle(); getState()==='error'.
entries.action: Run the one-off playwright check; document or collapse submit/submitAndSettle

entries.title: schema/verdict.json is unenforced until i3 — matches code by inspection only
entries.detail: Resolved: memory/writer.mjs now ajv-validates every write against schema/verdict.json (plus semantic checks), in-process and over HTTP. Residual schema looseness tracked separately as F11.

entries.title: Bedrock ranked list is not deduped — a duplicate name would be measured twice
entries.detail: picker.mjs `cleaned` keeps duplicates if Bedrock replies with a repeated candidate name; the loop would then benchmark that strategy twice. One-line fix: [...new Set(cleaned)].

entries.title: Bedrock live path never executed end-to-end (blocked on i7 credentials)
entries.detail: Offline fallback and error-fallback are verified; the success path (invoke, greedy \[.*\] regex parse of a possibly chatty reply, loser filter) is untested until AWS creds land. The greedy regex is the riskiest piece — mock-test it or tighten it before the i8 live capture.
entries.action: Exercise once creds land (i7); consider unit-mocking bedrockRank before then

entries.title: Benchmark environment changed: fresh Chromium 141 installed
entries.detail: Playwright's chromium binary was missing on this machine and was freshly installed (headless shell 141, playwright build v1194). Whatever environment produced the historical 6/6 PASS no longer exists here. Related to F1; also relevant to i6's CoV gate on Akash (pin the environment there).
entries.action: Recalibrate acceptance on THIS environment (with F1 decision); pin browser/CPU profile for i5/i6

entries.title: Demo start wipes the shared S3 scar file when the bucket env is set
entries.detail: scripts/demo.mjs awaits memory.reset() at startup so 'cold' is honest — but once BENCHFIRST_S3_BUCKET is set, that reset PUTs {} over the durable shared S3 memory. Fine for a solo demo; destructive if the scar file ever accumulates real cross-session history. Consider requiring an explicit --reset flag (or a separate demo key) before the i8 live capture.
entries.action: Decide reset semantics for the live (bucket-set) demo before i8

entries.title: S3 success path never executed end-to-end (blocked on i7 credentials)
entries.detail: Verified: local fallback round-trip, loud failure without credentials (CredentialsProviderError / missing region), corrupt-JSON rethrow, and NoSuchKey/NotFound-only cold start. Unverified until creds land: a real GET/PUT round-trip (including res.Body.transformToString on the locked SDK 3.1090) and the NoSuchBucket loud-throw. Also note: concurrent writers do last-write-wins (no ETag guard) — acceptable for the demo, worth knowing for i3.
entries.action: On creds day (i7): run one real round-trip + nonexistent-bucket test before trusting the warm story

entries.title: Noisy local benchmark can mint FALSE verdicts — observed live
entries.detail: Was: a noise-inflated baseline (CoV 0.348) let the cosmetic 'spinner' non-fix measure a fake 25.3% 'win' and reach memory. RESOLVED: optimizer/loop.mjs now has a determinism gate (MAX_COV=0.08, same bar as the Akash preflight). If the BASELINE CoV exceeds it the loop refuses early (report.untrusted=true, no verdict written); each CANDIDATE run is also guarded so a jittery candidate can't be promoted (beat=false + untrusted flag). Proven live during Step 1: a 0.265-CoV Page A run correctly REFUSED instead of writing — the same run that previously would have lied. Also fixed a crash the early-refuse path exposed (report.skippedKnownLosers now initialized on every path). See F15 for the machine-idle discipline that keeps CoV under the gate.
entries.action: Done. Pairs with F15 (keep the machine idle so honest runs don't trip the gate).

entries.title: schema/verdict.json (locked i0 interface) is looser than the writer's gate
entries.detail: The schema alone accepts: empty candidates {}, empty-string signature, and arbitrary rider fields (no additionalProperties:false) — audit verified a free-text 'opinion' field rides a valid verdict into the store. The writer compensates with code-level semantic checks (non-empty measurements, winner must have beat=true, usable signature key), but the schema itself can't be tightened unilaterally — it's a locked i0 interface, so tightening (minProperties, minLength, additionalProperties:false) needs Devansh's sign-off since his d1/d5 tasks build against it.
entries.action: Propose schema tightening at mentor review #1 / with Devansh; until then the writer's semantic layer is the real gate

entries.title: Writer binds hardcoded 127.0.0.1 — unreachable as a containerized Pomerium upstream
entries.detail: RESOLVED: startWriter now takes host from BENCHFIRST_WRITER_HOST (default 127.0.0.1 for local safety; containers set 0.0.0.0). Verified live: bound host echoed in the result and startup log. Still-open minor HTTP ergonomics (deferred, non-blocking, tracked here): unknown paths return 405 not 404; HTTP rejection replies flatten the errors array into the message; >1MB bodies get a connection reset instead of a 4xx — polish these when hardening for i5 if time permits.
entries.action: Bind host done. Optional HTTP-ergonomics polish remains for i5 hardening.

entries.title: 'Opinion can't reach S3' is convention until Pomerium + scoped credentials exist
entries.detail: In-process, any code that imports s3-store.mjs can still write directly, bypassing the writer (today only the writer and the demo's reset — known F8 — do). True enforcement arrives when i4 Pomerium fronts the HTTP writer with an identity policy AND i7 scopes S3 write credentials to the writer's identity only. The writer HTTP endpoint itself has no auth by design (auth is Pomerium's job).
entries.action: i4 + i7 close this; until then it's held by code review
entries.update_2026_07_18: i4 done — the identity gate now exists on TWO layers: (1) pomerium/config.yaml allow-policy admits exactly one identity to POST /verdict; (2) writer.mjs backstop rejects (403) any request whose x-pomerium-claim-email != BENCHFIRST_VERDICT_IDENTITY. Gate ON iff the env var is DEFINED; a defined-but-empty value FAILS CLOSED (refuses all writes) rather than silently opening (audit-hardened). healthz returns only {ok:true} — no bucket/key leak. Verified live: unset->200 (demo unchanged), empty->403, missing/wrong->403, correct->200. REMAINING to fully close: (a) i5 network isolation so the writer is reachable ONLY via Pomerium (else the backstop header could be set directly), (b) i7 scoped S3 creds (only the writer identity holds s3:PutObject), (c) the in-process bypass — the demo's direct s3-store.reset() (F8) still writes without the writer. Trust model + i7 checklist (verify pass_identity_headers emits x-pomerium-claim-email on the pinned Pomerium version; envsubst the ${ENV} placeholders) in pomerium/README.md + config.yaml.

entries.task: i6 preflight (run by Isha outside this session)
entries.title: Akash cannot host the benchmark — preflight CoV gate failed on every viable lease
entries.detail: Preflight harness (akash/preflight/, image ishamishra0408/akash-preflight:v2): local Mac CoV 0.029 PASS; Akash ZenCloud never booted; overclock-southwest 1CPU CoV 0.414 FAIL; overclock-west 4CPU CoV 0.178 FAIL — all far above the 0.08 gate. Resolution: Path B — benchmark runs on Isha's Mac (the proven 0.029 environment); Akash instead hosts the WRITER + Pomerium, so the trust story becomes 'write-path enforced on decentralized compute'. The three CoV numbers are pitch evidence: the foundation was tested before being built on.
entries.action: None — decision made and documented; revised i5/i7 execute Path B

entries.title: The Mac's 0.029 CoV was measured IDLE — under load this same machine hit 0.348
entries.detail: Path B promotes the local Mac to official benchmark host on the strength of preflight CoV 0.029. But during this session's demo runs (builds, downloads, browsers running), baseline CoV on the same Mac reached 0.348 and minted a false verdict (F10). The 0.029 number is conditional on an idle machine. For the live i8 capture: close other apps, don't run builds mid-demo, and (per F10's recommendation) gate verdicts on measured CoV so a noisy run refuses rather than lies.
entries.action: Adopt an idle-machine checklist for demo runs + the F10 local CoV gate
entries.update_2026_07_18: F10 gate now SHIPPED (a noisy run refuses instead of lying), so the downside is a false NEGATIVE (honest run trips the gate), not a false verdict. Confirmed live in Step 1: Page A baseline CoV swung 0.265 (loaded, refused) -> 0.002 (idle, passed) across back-to-back runs with load avg ~4 on a 10-core Mac, Docker Desktop up. Observed sub-pattern: Page A baseline is STRUCTURALLY noisier than Page B (Page A CoV 0.24-0.35 vs Page B 0.005-0.017 across the session) — likely Page A's 7 per-fee re-renders + 'Amount Due' bump animation (key={running}) vs Page B's 5 cards. For the demo: quit Docker Desktop + heavy apps first; if a run refuses, just re-run. Consider (with Devansh) smoothing Page A's per-fee re-render if it keeps tripping.

entries.resolved: 2026-07-19 — Devansh confirmed the Path B updates; plan text updated
entries.title: docs/build-plan.html still describes Path A for i5/i6/i7
entries.detail: RESOLVED: Isha synced Devansh on the Path B updates and he confirmed. build-plan.html updated — i0-i6 marked done; i5 now 'containerize the WRITER (not harness), writer exposed only to Pomerium'; i6 now 'preflight proved Akash can't hold CoV<=0.08, bench runs local'; i7 now 'deploy writer+Pomerium, no CoV requirement, go-live kit ready'. Devansh's d6 pitch framing (Pomerium verdict-only write) already Path-B-compatible, no change needed.

entries.title: Pomerium half of the pair can't be booted or tested locally — needs i7 IdP + secrets
entries.detail: i5 containerized the WRITER and proved it end-to-end in Docker (build -> run -> healthz {ok:true}, POST no-identity 403, wrong-identity 403, correct-identity 200, opinion 400, GET 405, docker HEALTHCHECK healthy). But real Pomerium requires an IdP + cookie/shared secrets + signing key, none of which exist until i7 — so the docker-compose `pomerium` service sits under a `full` profile and the Akash SDL's pomerium service is structurally complete but unrun. What is UNVERIFIED until i7: that Pomerium actually boots with pomerium/config.yaml, that pass_identity_headers emits x-pomerium-claim-email, and that the writer is reachable ONLY via Pomerium (network isolation). The gate is proven at the writer layer, asserted (not yet demonstrated) at the proxy layer.
entries.action: i7: fill secrets/IdP, boot Pomerium against an echo upstream to confirm the header name, then run the full pair on an Akash lease

entries.title: Writer image is amd64-pinned in FROM; unverified assumptions for the live deploy
entries.detail: akash/writer/Dockerfile pins FROM --platform=linux/amd64 (Docker lint warns; deliberate — Akash providers are x86_64, matches the preflight image). Built + ran on the Apple-Silicon Mac via emulation (317MB image, boots fine). Unverified until i7/deploy: (a) the image running natively on an amd64 Akash provider; (b) the Akash SDL's service-to-service expose (`writer` exposed only `to: [service: pomerium]`) actually isolates the writer from global ingress on the chosen provider; (c) resource profile (0.5 cpu / 256Mi writer, 0.5 / 512Mi pomerium) is enough for Pomerium. Push the image (i7) before deploy — the SDL references ishamishra0408/benchfirst-writer:v1 which is not yet published.
entries.action: i7: docker push the writer image; verify service-to-service isolation + Pomerium resources on the live lease

entries.title: i7 live provisioning is blocked on credentials — kit is ready, execution is yours
entries.detail: This session has NO AWS creds, no aws/Akash CLI, no docker login — the live half of i7 (S3 bucket, Bedrock access, image push, Pomerium/IdP, Akash lease) can only be done by Isha on the consoles. What Step 4 delivered instead, all offline-verified: (1) build-plan updated to Path B (F16); (2) .env.example — full env contract; (3) .gitignore hardened so .env/.env.*/*.pem can't be committed; (4) scripts/verify-live.mjs (npm run verify-live) — a preflight that turns F6/F9/F17/F18 into one runnable check, each leg SKIPs when its env is unset (proven: offline = all SKIP exit 0; against a local gated writer = Writer-gate PASS); (5) docs/GO-LIVE.md — the ordered, credential-gated runbook. Nothing live has been executed or verified end-to-end yet.
entries.action: Isha: install aws + provider-services CLIs, then walk docs/GO-LIVE.md stage by stage, running `npm run verify-live` after each. This closes F6, F9, F17, F18.

entries.title: README documented runInteraction() -> { ms } but code returns { ms, settleModelMs }
entries.detail: One-line doc drift, fixed in README.md:70.

entries.title: Dual source of truth for candidate ordering (loop inline copy vs picker.mjs)
entries.detail: i1 wiring removed the inline block; loop.mjs now calls pickOrder(). Audit verdict SHIP; offline order proven identical.

entries.title: Dead initializer skippedKnownLosers:[] in loop report
entries.detail: Immediately overwritten after the pickOrder call; removed.
